mentions.text:
@zephyr_z9 So that memory demand statement is true, but it's not the focus of the companies listed in the article. 

They've largely left the market YMTC/CXMT is trying to flood to go after the higher margin hyperscaler AI buildout, so little to no effect on companies like MIcron. 

If anything it would hurt legacy void type companies like Nanya + Winbond, which should have been the focus. 

Naunce is Micron, Sk Hynix, and Samsung have largely  hollowed out their demand from China (eg. ~10% micron, ~15-18% Sk hynix), and shifted dram wafers to hbm3e and hbm4.  Maybe only Samsung is slightly affected given their share with mobile/consumer devices.
